title: Invalid internal status after reboot
body:
### Issue Description

After restarting my server I get this error.
WARN[0000] "/" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers 
ERRO[0000] invalid internal status, try resetting the pause process with "podman system migrate": could not find any running process: no such process 

Following system migration the containers hang in an improper state and I can't stop them. I have to rm -f them and use their run commands again.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. reboot
2. podman ps
WARN[0000] "/" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers 
ERRO[0000] invalid internal status, try resetting the pause process with "podman system migrate": could not find any running process: no such process 
3. podman system migrate
stopped 09ca96f8a4607e11dd5f84fbe8d60e4cecbaac1d94411b87d2661c9957d65922
5. podman ps -a
WARN[0000] "/" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers 
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                 COMMAND     CREATED        STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
09ca96f8a460  docker.io/syncthing/syncthing:latest              4 minutes ago  Stopping                syncthing

### Describe the results you received

Podman state improper/ containers hang in stopping state

### Describe the results you expected

podman lists my stopped containers for me to start them/autostarts them

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Your tmpdir and runroot must be on tmpfs or it at least it must be removed on each boot, so remove
`/tmp/containers-user-1000` and `/tmp/podman-run-1000` and it should work.

@Luap99 I would think that Red Hat should modify their provided `/usr/lib/tmpfiles.d/podman.conf` in order to clean up /tmp/containers-user-* and /tmp/podman-run-* at each boot.

I just now put the following in place in `/etc/tmpfiles.d/podman-local.conf` that I'm expecting to solve the issue reported above.
```
# Nuke podman ephemeral stuff to avoid issues where /tmp is not of type tmpfs and persists across reboots.
# The format of this file is defined in the tmpfiles.d(5) man page. The "!" modifier specifies that the action
# should happen only at boot.
#
# Type Path Mode User Group Age Argument
R! /tmp/containers-user-*
R! /tmp/podman-run-*
```

I had to run also `podman system reset` for rootless user
Beware that this will remove the containers and the podman secrets!
